name: AWS Lambda Powertools (Lambda Best Practices Library)

auth.notes: Uses Lambda execution role; no separate auth

performance.notes: Minimal overhead; designed for Lambda cold start sensitivity

agent_readiness.notes: AWS's official Lambda best practices library. Tracer (@tracer.capture_lambda_handler — X-Ray). Logger (@logger.inject_lambda_context — structured JSON). Metrics (@metrics.log_metrics — CloudWatch EMF). Event Source Data Classes: APIGatewayProxyEvent, SQSEvent, etc. Idempotency utility (DynamoDB-backed). Parameters utility (SSM/Secrets Manager/AppConfig). Batch processing (SQS/Kinesis/DynamoDB Streams). TypeScript version: @aws-lambda-powertools/tracer. The must-have Lambda library.

gotchas: AWS Lambda only (not for other serverless platforms)
Tracer requires X-Ray active tracing enabled on function
Python and TypeScript versions maintained separately
best_when: Any production AWS Lambda function (Python or TypeScript)
Structured logging to CloudWatch with request correlation IDs
SQS/Kinesis batch processing with partial failure handling
avoid_when: Non-AWS Lambda environments (Azure Functions, GCP Cloud Functions)
Lambda functions where cold start size critical (it adds ~10MB)
Simple one-off scripts deployed as Lambda
summary: AWS Lambda Powertools is the official library for Lambda best practices. @logger.inject_lambda_context for structured JSON logging. @tracer.capture_lambda_handler for X-Ray tracing. @metrics.log_metrics for CloudWatch custom metrics. Event data classes (APIGatewayProxyEvent, SQSEvent). Idempotency utility. Batch processing. The must-have library for every production Python or TypeScript Lambda function.
